title: biscutella coronopifolia var. glareosa
intro: Biscutella coronopifolia var. glareosa is a rare and lesser-known variety of the Biscutella coronopifolia plant, a type of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae, commonly known as the corn-marigold or biscutella. This plant is prized for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Biscutella coronopifolia var. glareosa include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used as a tea, or made into a tincture, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
